channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >AIIIIIEEEEEEE DON'T SELL — 50 replies — 200.0/hr

>AIIIIIEEEEEEE DON'T SELL

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-03-03 19:23 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61924202
>>61924190

He literally said it kek

61924210
>>61924190

>do not sell
Wait what?

61924222
>>61924190

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61924452
>>61924190
i trust anything this man says after what happened the day he posted pic related

61924459
>>61924350
He doesn't think deeply enough to consider consequences

61924479
>>61924412
He thought this will end like Venezuela. Cut off the head and be done and reap in all the applause.
But the Ayatollah was already 87 that mfer could have died every day due to his age anyways so Iran was prepared

61924494
>>61924190
My son longed crypto.
Thank you for your attention to this matter!

61924513
>>61924270
>someone smother this fucking demented old retard
Rubio's too much of a cuck for that.

61924515
>>61924452

61924522
>>61924513
He's too short to reach over the top of the bed

61924558
Selling is now a federal offense according to some law Trump found from 1813.

61924600
Greatest president in history. Getting rid of evil Jihadists while keeping the stock market pumping.

Bless DJT

61924637
>day 3 of the 4 day special regime change operation

61924674
>>61924479
>The Ayatollah literally had 42 leaders prepared in case the Jews killed the first 41 successors at the same time as khamenei
I'm sure the 42nd guy down the line is going to be a great leader and will really stand up to a nation that spends double his country's GDP on their military alone.

61924691
>>61924190
DO NOT REDEEEEM

61924712
>>61924259
Reminder that dodging the draft is based and only niggers, faggots, and white trash join the (((military))).

61924715
>>61924637

61924755
>>61924674

i thought /biz/ would understand basic economic concepts

61924762

61924893

61924904

but then he is legitimately a senile moron who never read a book soooo... idk.

61926608
>>61924190
DO NOT SELL
STOP SELLING
YOU WILL FUCK THE ECONOMY REEEEE

61926626
>>61924270
Cope, seethe, dial8, YWNBAW et cetera. Oh, and 3 more years. Oh oh, and I voted for this.

61927505
>>61924479
>>61924674
Iran has spent the past 30 years preparing for this day, for them it's existential, it's the reason they wanted nukes. They may ultimately be destroyed but they are not going to surrender or show restraint.

61928137
>>61924190
One month special military operation

61928146
>>61924237

Why do zoomers say the most retarded shit, ah hell we were all young and dumb once I guess.

61928151

61928154

USA LOSS THE WAR

item.title: PYPL merger edition 1 — 89 replies — 142.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
PYPL merger edition 1

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-03-03 18:49 UTC

OP

Here we go!

Early replies (first 3)

61891698
Do it Ryan.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61904711
>>61904697
HOLY FUUUUCK
OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

61904727
>>61891742
>Ripple Labs will buy PayPal. Screen Cap this.
I don't think so, only because they claimed they didn't have any big acquisitions planned for the rest of the year

61908061
>>61903001
739M SHARE

61909665
no merger.
we are fucked

61909693
>>61909665
the gap will be filled

61909824
>>61904697

61910089
>>61906351
Thanks for playing, baggies.

61910124
>>61909824

61915327
Any news?

61916113
>>61915327
https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/filing-intech-investment-management-llc-purchases-30734-shares-of-paypal-holdings-inc-pypl-2026-02-28/

61918275
Is this still going to 60$? I went all in at 46 because of this thread.

61919240
>>61918275
I Hope return at price of institutional have buy

61919338
>>61892826
>$300
impressive

61919350
>>61918275
>d
the CEO will get his big cut only at $83 dollar share price MINIMUM.
look at warner bros that shit went from below $10 to now beeing baught at $30
If Paypal gets aquired it will be atleast $100

61919773
>>
https://hedgefollow.com/stocks/PYPL
Hope

61921315
>>61919350
I doubt it will go that high.

61923340
>>61923318

61923342
>>61923340

61925981
>>61904727
At the rate they were going, it wasn't going to be a big acquisition, merely a ripple on the pond

61926795
AMZN

61927102
Can someone explain why Paula even exists?? I remember you needed it for eBay back when everything was new. Somehow it morphed into the default asking to payment

61928616
>>61927102
It exists because easy cc payment processor services didn't exist for a long time. You used to give your cc info directly to the web vendors at payment, which eventually got people scammed on shady websites. Paypal was the first of its kind there. The internet has somehow COMPLETELY forgotten that money used to be a complete pain in the ass to move around. Btc, being somewhat clunky now, was actually a huge move in being able to transfer money without oversight or other friction overhead. Now that its been incorporated into the collectivist panopticon, both btc and PayPal are harder to use than just years ago

61930361
ooooooooooooooooooooooooooo

61931059
46,75$
+2,75%

item.title: Hyperliquid >decentralized perp and spot exchange >trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war >genius l… — 41 replies — 82.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hyperliquid >decentralized perp and spot exchange >trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war >genius l…

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-03-03 18:57 UTC

OP

Hyperliquid

>decentralized perp and spot exchange
>trading silver, gold and crude oil on weekends during a war
>genius level founders. Harvard, MIT and caltech.
>no vc funding from day 1
>31% geneis airdop tokens to users minting millionaires for early usrs.
>97% all platfrom fees spent and buy HYPE tokens from open market and sent to burned wallet.


Holding HYPE is comfy AF

Early replies (first 3)

61918612
>>61918597
Buy an ad.
CLEAN IT UP JANNIES

61918623
>>61918597
>Shilled by literally all of Twitter fags including scammers like Arthur
>Token not needed
Yeah, no.

61918628
>>61918623
why don't people say "stock not needed". its a crypto company and the coin is basically the stock.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61919354
>>61919323
Is the hype token used as gas for the chain?

61919376
Fuck hyperliquid, they forced cz the good guy to giganuke the market (like he did in 2022 to destroy ftx/sbf), but these fuckers are still alive.
They need to disappear so cz can allow the market to recover.

61919383
>>61919376
I don't hold any hype but I use the exchange on a daily basis and it works better than even centralized exchanges. It's the best product out there.

61919391
>>61919354
Yes. Hype is used as gas on the HyperEVM chain.

There are two chains in Hyperliquid ecosystem.

HyperCore - trading engine. No gas needed. Pay with trading fees which is used to buy and burn HYPE.

HyperEVM - smart-contract layer. HYPE used as bas.

61920112
I was thinking about grabbing a bag in late Jan and then it pumped again. Serves me right for wanting to buy this under 20 burgerbux

61920124
>>61920112
Same, bro. I just hold btc and trade on hyperliquid. I wanted to get myself a good back of hype, it's the best project out there and the team are not niggers. They want hype to go up. This weekends pump is because everybody is using it to trade crypto and commodities.

One of the few chains that is actually used. No marketing, no bullshit kantian philosophy or any kind of german idealism talk like Sergey or Charles hoskinson

61920169
How do I even buy Hype in the US? I need to use a VPN?

61920469
>>61919376

61920537
can i trade spot btc on there?

61920572
>>61920537
yes. Crypto, stocks, commodities... both spot and perps

61920574
Forex too

61921651
>>61920169
it's on Coinbase now starting Feb 2026. Not sure if it's available in USA.

VPN is very useful. expressvpn

61921686
Funny , this thread popped up because hype's about the moon

61921966
>>61918612
tell that to bloomberg you stupid nigger

61921981
>>61918623
>hurr im not going to buy this multi-billion marketcap token because [insert random person i dont like] is in it!!!
You are thinking like a pleb here and will simply miss out on profits this way.

61922017
>>61918597
I only have 250 hype and refuse to buy up here. It has to retrace back to the low 20's at some point.

Also shilling hype here is a waste. This entire board is so underwater on their bags they're in the mariana trenches.
>Link generals
>Avi generals
>BBBY/GME

I said this to myself earlier this year but Jeff and Vitalik are truly the last 2 people in this entire fucking space that still hasn't soul their souls yet

61922034
>>61921981
Look up GMX.

61924490
Sigh
These poor souls

61924506
edgex is better

61924534
>>61921981
why would he want to buy something that is already multibillions?

61924803
>>61918597
>sent to burned wallet.

why are you lying? the tokens arent burned, they are kept in their treasury

they probably will never have a need to touch those tokens, but they arent burned

61924887
>>61918707
So, the gas is, on a direct level paid in USDC. This is why you one-time sign, and can trade without having to sign each time, unlike GMX. Your trading fees are gas.
These trading fees are used to buy & burn hype. That's why the exchange is a fee collecting engine, and purchasing a share in said engine is by buying hype, which also serves as governance.

Also /biz/ is retarded these days, one-shotted by chainlink and not knowing about anything outside of the cult bubble.

61924972
>>61918597
>source, closed

61926502
>>61922017
it literally broke 20 in the january drop , it should be unhindered to high 40s or 50 into a quick retest of idk 35ish into ath or just straight to 60 over some weeks/months.

61926525
>>61924803
it's 2026. Ask AI about HYPE burn address and stop making shit up.

61927011
>>61926502
Nah this is moonboy posting. The regulatory and closed source fud are real and people are quick to point to the 11 man team making trillions of revenue a year as a bullish moat when any logical investor sees that as a massive target for competitors.

The team at least recognizes this hence the $28 million funding a lobbying team for US compliance. But on a strict regulatory angle Lighter edges Hype out simply due to the fact they're structured from the ground up as a US company on top of their VC connections

61927038
>>61927011
must be great for the lighter shareholders

61929634
>>61927011
The VC connections are precisely the problem with Lighter. Half the damn supply belongs to the team and VCs. Less than 3k people hold this shit and most of the circulating supply is in the top 10. Only 25% of supply is circulating. FDV is through the roof and it's done nothing but dump since it was launched. Once it's -85% it might be worth a look but not before

61930154
>>61918597
It’s got only another 10x left in it, 30x if generalized vm takes off
Bretty good with size
I will buy a bag if I see it trading near $15 later this year

61931112
>>61926525
yea i see, in dec they voted to treat the system address as a burn address and never touch it.

still not technically burned, but functionally burned as long as another vote doesnt pass to loot the wallet(which isnt possible with a proper burn)

anyone know a proper site to track daily burns?

item.title: WTF IS THIS JEWISH SHIT? — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTF IS THIS JEWISH SHIT?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-03-03 19:26 UTC

OP

i invested 5k on a parlay that we will be in a war with Iran by the end of the year. CLEARLY we are in a war. But since the State department says we are not, my parlay doesn't HIT? WTF

Early replies (first 3)

61931033
get fucked gambler

61931036
>>61931026
You are dealing with a race people who invented a special light switch so that they can trick God himself

61931046
>>61931026
This is fucking obvious though congress are the only ones with the power to declare war? Its just another trick he uses so that he can be Israel greatest solider

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61931050
>>61931026
I voted for this

61931054
>>61931026
Trump has been saying he's not into starting wars for like 10 years now. If you bet against that you deserve what you get, sorry but Dems are projecting as usual.

61931056
Get fucked tranny. Not a war just because MSDNC says so

61931064
>>61931033
BTC and Stox are literally gambling faggot

61931065
>>61931026
What Market did get affected by this?

61931082
>>61931026
Lol, you're trusting Jews and pajeets to make an unbiased call where money is concerned

61931156
>>61931026
I think 1942 was the last time we formally declared war. America is a nation of peace.

61931183
So everyone made fun of russia's special military operation and now they do exactly the same? Clown world

61931227
>>61931183
Trump's getting raped in the midterms and he's finally getting clobbered in the courts too.

61931237
>>61931026
you are stupid as fuck for betting on things that have open ended answers rather than very clear conditions

61931253
>>61931183
He's repeating all Putin's talking points too lmao
Even did the whole "we're not even using our full power" bit

61931274
>Betting on something where the rules and definitions are vague and can be changed whenever or declared whatever they want at any time
Retards. Might as well just hang yourself up in a kosher slaughterhouse.

item.title: Why the FUCK is war bullish now? — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why the FUCK is war bullish now?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-03-03 19:23 UTC

OP

Why the FUCK is war bullish now?

Early replies (first 3)

61924155
>now
go back to /pol/ retard
/biz/ is a neocon board

61924177
>>61924139
Newfag?

61924229
>>61924139
How new are you

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61924353
>>61924139
>investing is jewish
>jews start all wars
>thus wars are bullish

61924355
Pedophile rapists market buy whatever they own with fake monopoly money in order to make themselves richer. Once you understand this everything makes sense

61927592
>>61924139
refugees is bullish

61927712
>>61924155
this board hates war

61927717
>>61924139
Same reason layoffs are bullish for companies. Cleaning up the deadweight

61927721
>>61924155
>ten years after the start of a war
I think people are more concerned with the next few weeks.

61928006
the only THEY can realistically sell is food shelter and fertile virgins

61930872
>>61927712
no it doesn't

61931163
>>61924139
Because USA/Israel/Zog/Nato empire is about to install a new "democratic and free" regime in Iran and receive 92.4 million new "Costumers" for it's FED fiat dollar ponzi-scheme.

61931226
>>61924355
^This.
Once you fully understand that all geopolitical and military decisions of USA/NATO/ZOG are based on delaying the FIAT/FED/USD ponzi scheme from breaking all decisions will make sense.
This is why Russia preemptively struck Ukraine, as once Ukraine joins NATO and gets sucked dry Russia is one of the next potential targets, and Russia chose it's preferable front for defense.
Nobody want's to be at the bottom of a ponzi scheme.

61931250
Iran is buying bitcoin, they're the only reason we stopped dumping.

61931260
>>61931226
Kek why do you Ivans still patrol these dead boards?

item.title: What's the best high yield savings account right now? — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's the best high yield savings account right now?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-03 19:23 UTC

OP

What's the best high yield savings account right now? Mine just went from 4% to 3.3% APY, so looking to change

Early replies (first 3)

61930464
>>61930455
idk

61930660
>>61930455
marcus has some cds that are at or near 4, some are no penalty

61930673
>>61930455
It doesn't matter, savings accounts are a meme. You can make 4% in a day with one good trade.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61930724
>>61930673
>just roll the dice my guy, it'll pay off.
what a stupid fucking nigger

61930844
>>61930673
you can also make -20% with one bad trade, which isn’t possible with a hysa

61931040
>>61930455
swvvx is a money market mutual fund that was paying 4.12% last week, but it fuctuates.

I keep my "free cash" in that just so it will be available within 24 hours if I need it.

For high yield, I have a basket of MREITS that pay crazy monthly divvies but I rotate through them, buying them when they are low and holding.

My favorite is DX - pays like 15% but I only buy when it is under $12.50 and it is around 14 now.

Also: GLAD - pays 9% and is at 52 week lows so just sold some DUKE (3.5% dividend) because it is up 30% from where I bought it and am rolling that into GLAD

Gladstone has a suite of financing companies and a REIT so you can check them out. They are fairly stable over time and pay around 7-10% each.

I think these financing companies are a good buy right now because the lend on long duration (often with pre-payment penalties) and borrow at short duration. This means that if rates drop, they will still be getting 6-9% on their loans, but paying 1-3% on their funds Since their capital is leveraged like 90%, that means they are actually making like 25-40% RoC.

61931043
The best high yield savings account to use is a brokerage and sgov or some other money market fund.

61931066
>>61931040
>>61931043
Does it matter that there's no FDIC insurance on these compared to a HYSA?

61931085
>>61930455
A CD

61931106
>>61931066
yes it matters.

that's why you are getting a high yield.
>risk = >interest/divs/etc

61931133
>>61930455
https://www.pibank.com/pibank-savings/
4.6%, doubt it’ll last long though

61931195
>>61930455
Blackrock ishare etfs, you can choose the term length of what bonds are in the etf. Schwab swvvx sucks because it pays so erratically.

61931216
>>61931066
SGOV is literally backed by the US military

61931263
>>61931106
>>61931216
Hmm looks like sgov is exempt from state income tax? That's even more money saved.

>risk
is it that risky if it's backed by short term US T-Bills? I would think it's crazy safe, especially for just keeping some money around for dips, maybe like $50k tops.

>>61931040
hmm looks like the dividends are fully taxable at the state level.

>>61931085
but then you're locking your money up. You give up liquidity.

>>61931133
found picrel for 4.75% FDIC insured. Is there any real catch with these banks? I have Sofi and never had an issue while getting 4-4.5% APY the last year.

>>61931195
yeah, these look good for a long term hold, but how do the returns compare to sgov? Looks like sgov is more flexible, but might do a mix of both

61923468

61924182

item.title: The megabanks are scooping up all the BTC and ETH and you're doing nothing to stop them. — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The megabanks are scooping up all the BTC and ETH and you're doing nothing to stop them.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-03 19:12 UTC

OP

The megabanks are scooping up all the BTC and ETH and you're doing nothing to stop them.

Early replies (first 3)

61930669
>>61930657
they do, fuck

61930675
Pretty sure bitcoin would be $15k if MSTR was buying. I see banks buying $10m here and there, but that’s nothing. Wake me up in 2040 when bitcoin is $500k. This is exhausting.

61930686
>>61930675
Wasn’t* buying. Bitcoin will continue to go up over time, but it’s going to be a long, bumpy ride.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61930749
>>61930657
I think they are. Which is kind of retarded because it was going to 40k and they're accumulating at 60.

61931045
>>61930675
>btc is only big because of [visible entity]!!!1!
opinion retarded and disregarded
go back

61931048
>>61930657
Cuz I'm still pretty sure I can get them a lot cheaper than this

61931145
>>61930657
They’re buying it on behalf of their clients who are then immediately dumping it.

61931192
we gonna sell the news once the clarity act is passed?

item.title: WHAT WAS THE POINT OF THE PRECIOUS METALS FAD? — 7 replies — 23.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHAT WAS THE POINT OF THE PRECIOUS METALS FAD?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-03-03 19:08 UTC

OP

Now that the dust has settled, PMs were all rugged and rock collectors are back to holding bags under the mountain, can we all agree that this entire PM fad was entirely dumb?

Gold and silver achieved nothing besides under-leveraging bitcoin and leading to unecessary panic liquidations as whales swapped BTC for rocks. The reason why PMs even rose in value to instigate such curiosity if they'd be the next bitcoin was probably China, Russia and Brazil making simultaneous big acquisitions. Now, as they need dollars to buy oil from more primary sources, they are probably selling because nobody trades in healthy currencies. PM fags do not understand the concept of reserve value at all, and think people would

Only cryptocurrency is:
>discrete
>instantaneous
>universal
>free of government intervention

Now PM fags stand corrected, since they probably would've been better buying Pokémon cards than their rocks.

PM prices are an accurate measure of how much leverage the mentally unwell hold in the economy.

Early replies (first 3)

61930467
>>61930217
>Now that the dust has settled, PMs were all rugged and rock collectors are back to holding bags
Gold and Silver are up >10% YTD while your favorite shitcoin lost a quarter of its value in the same timeframe.

61930516
>fad

61930640
>>61930467
>YTD

Zoom out.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61930789
>>61930640
>Zoom out.
I fail to see how that helps your case.

61931092
>>61930789
Zoom in, goy

61931120
>>61930467
dont worry your useless rocks are going back to $20 soon enough

61931171
Only reason I hold silver is to make kikes like OP seethe
Don't even care about the (((price)))

item.title: I just found out about “The Work Number” It’s Equifax’s wagie permanent record where all of your work history and salary… — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I just found out about “The Work Number” It’s Equifax’s wagie permanent record where all of your work history and salary…

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-03-03 19:24 UTC

OP

I just found out about “The Work Number”
It’s Equifax’s wagie permanent record where all of your work history and salary information is listed. It’s what most HR departments use to verify employment history and your pay when applying to a new job
>mfw I now realize I can’t quit a job because it goes on some gay permanent record I can’t lie about

The US is really a dystopian shithole lmao

Early replies (first 3)

61931254
Probably the only list I’m not on kek

61931255
>>61931247
>the it's going on your permanent record meme is real

61931268
>>61931247
they don't. it's for minimum wagie extremes. real blue collar white collar jobs don't get listed there unless they are mcfranchisetm. focus on crafting your resume around overselling to the fags

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Food is so cheap it is practically free. — 107 replies — 135.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Food is so cheap it is practically free.

Open thread • Replies: 107 • Updated: 2026-03-03 18:39 UTC

OP

The image posted is in AUD, but for those in the US, Lets assume an hourly wage of $15.

$15 USD in the US can buy 25lbs of rice. which is 45,000 calories. that is astounding.

I work minimum wage (AUS) and after 2 hours of work I can afford an entire weeks worth of food.

the first hour affords me actually nearly a months worth of carbohydrate energy (rice, pasta and potato's)

The second hour affords me 3kg of meat. which is more than enough protein for a week.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Staple_food

very helpful link to understand there is actually not that much food in the entire world to eat for bulk calories/nutrition. there is about 7 different foods to eat. (wheat, rice, corn, potato, meat, dairy and bananas/plantains in certain regions, honorable mention for beans and lentils as well.)

Early replies (first 3)

61926835
People think they're too good for rice and beans, supplemented with fresh vegetables. They deserve to be poor. For the price of two colas you can buy an entire bag of beans which will feed you for weeks. Stop eating corposlop made in a lab that's marked up 10000%, eat real food.

61926840
>>61926822
>eating is so cheap when you eat like a 13th century

61926847
>>61926822
>food is so heckin cheap when you eat like a medieval Chinese peasant!
>updoots to the left!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61927498
>>61927022
You are a nigger and a jew.

61927603
>>61926835
Not to mention, if you're gonna waste your dopamine receptors, you might as well be creampie-ing hookers on ecstasy instead of eating cheeseburgers.

61927622
>>61926835

Based. Eating rice, beans and scrambled eggs is peak. Poorfags be cuck'd for the mc'ds tho. Wondering why they poor n tummy still upset. Sad.

61927677
>>61926822
Switched to keto 3 days ago.
I'm done with carbslop.
Rice and beans alone are mestizo feed and give you long term health issues.
Surprisingly it isn't costing me more than $5 a day, but I tend to buy things on discount.

61927697
food was absurdly cheap pre covid. 12 dollar lunch buffets. the 1 dollar mcchicken. sausage meats starting at 1 dollar per lb. that shit was so cheap i was buying them to feed to my raccoons.

now its more expensive but the basics are still pretty good. minimum wage can buy 2-4 dozen eggs per hour.

61927747
>>61926840
>>61926847
Rice with chicken and beans is infinitely more nutritious than Mcdonalds. Cry about it you are weak. Cheap food cooked well is the most beneficial.

61927938
>>61926835
>rice and beans, supplemented with fresh vegetables
this and tuna/deenz+salad is top tier cuisine
As a student I survived so well on this. Best food ever.

61928034
>>61927193
Soil has no very little to do with nutrients. Genetics of the plant has more control over nutrients, so the long lasting bland food that was bred to be that way is the problem. Having regional markets and actual seasons for strains of vegetables that taste good and don't have a long shelf life would contribute to having more nutrients in food.

61928068
>>61926848
Mountain Dew

61928075
>>61926822
>>61926835
Holy fuck. Irredeemably delusional.
Eating only rice and beans like one day you will wake up and have a meltdown about why you did that stupid overly frugal living shit to yourself. That is not gonna be a good day.

61928215
>>61926822
the chemicals and fake meat in Mcmuttslop are literal poison, at least in europe they force these evil fucks to make real food

61928231
>>61926822
Okay great, doesn't matter though when rent is your largest expense by a huge margin

61928338
>>61926867
fpbp

61928341
>>61926867
6 billion pajeets and gooks suggest otherwise

61928406
>>61926822
>calories

Amazing maths there. I better start drinking sunflower oil instead of eating food - that has twice the calories of rice! Just 11lbs of that already beats your 45000 calories bag of rice. Astounding

61928427
>>61926835
>vegetables
anything that isn't potatoes/carrot/cabbage/onion is expensive

61928442
>>61926822
>>61926835
>>61926870
Based. Most first world people just don't have any frugal skills. They fall for the consumer slop and blame there low wage for being broke.

61928596
>>61928075
You don't need to eat beans and rice, you can eat egg rice with minced meat, that's cheap as shit too. Or something like potato with fried chicken. Or Caesar salad. All things you can easily make yourself that cost very little

61928700
>>61926822
McDonalds is garbage
Rice has no nutrition

61928746
>>61927442
Fair enough if you want me to be completely specific, because it really impossible to consume muscle meat without consuming fat, it is always connected unless you are a freak that cuts it off.
But to concede semantics, you need the muscle meat and associated fat, in a ratio of around 70-30 more or less respectively. Organs aren't required, they are nutritious, sometimes overly nutritious to a toxic level in excess.
I don't eat them mainly because they taste nasty. My bones aren't disintigrating, my muscles still grow stronger through training, my shits are 100 times better than before and I have no debilitating chronic diseases. N =1 but it just works.
To keep it /biz/ related, this diet is incredibly cheap and simple and still satiating and appetizing, and I can't recommend it enough. It won't be as cheap as beans and rice but also you won't be sick, constipated, low energy, brain fogged, skinny fat grain slave that ends up spending 200k in hospital bills and medicines.
I spend $400 a month on food, but I do get the much more expensive grass fed ground beef and special cuts from a local farm because I can afford it. If you are in a pinch, those 70/30 beef tubes from walmart could easily get you to survive with minimal effort and probably only $150 a month or so.

61928773
>>61928700
Rice is ok. I wasted way too much of my life studying biochemistry.

Its kinda shit to have incomplete proteins with low bioavailability. You want to have like 1 gram per pound of bodyweight of protein per day. Ideally Cysteine & Taurine.

Look into the cuts of meat we stopped eating in like the late 1800s/early 1900s, these ones are cheap and actually have what we need

61928775
>>61926835
>People think they're too good for rice and beans,
yes, I am rich.

61928813
>>61926822
it's never been easier to avoid eating slop because as well as raising the prices so much food companies also have degraded the quality of ingredients in many snacks and processed foods so they don't taste good anymore.

61928855
>>61927677
retard, carbs are key for energy and recovery, you just have to learn to eat the right amount at the right time, 100g rice 2-3 hours before lifting, 40g rice after lifting to restore glycogen stores, lower carbs at dinner, etc…

61928879
>>61928855
>retard, carbs are key for energy and recovery,
(in your opinion)
after a few months of adaptation, 0 carb btfo carb loading in virtually every single scenario, along with other added benefits. Carbs are just trash. Even animals that eat only plants like cows convert their food into fatty acids through fermentation.

61928887
>>61928773

My go to lunch bowl is rice + eggs + peas (or something else). Very cheap and basically macronutritionally complete.

61928919
>>61926822
>t. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_pDTiFkXgEE

61930720
Feds on 4chan teaching chuds how to cook to prevent collapse wasn't on my 2026 bingo card

61930744
For me, it's oats. They contain:
>Magnesium (muscle and nerve function)
>Iron (oxygen transport)
>Zinc (immune health)
>B vitamins (energy metabolism)
>Manganese (bone formation, metabolism)
>Protein (more than most grains)
>Contains avenanthramides (unique antioxidants in oats)

61930997
As a Dutchman for me it's potatoes, meat, vegetables.

item.title: Day 1652 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M — 10 replies — 40.0/hr

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61927155
>>61927118

61927206
no buys

no sells

is this the end of the s curve

61927222
>>61927118

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61927296

61930023
>>61927118

61930375
>>61927118

61930817

>Day 1650

item.title: STRC now pays 11.5% dividend. — 31 replies — 37.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
STRC now pays 11.5% dividend.

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-03-03 18:36 UTC

OP

Is there anyone who thinks that this shit is going to end well?

Early replies (first 3)

61924371
>he isn't looping STRC
ngmi

61924448
>>61924371
Holy shit is finally the end of Bitcoin...

61924465
>>61924170
Strategy's debt is structured quite defensively. Almost all social media commentary completely misses the point because those lazy asses don't even look into how the company an its products work.
STRC in particular pays an adaptable, voluntary dividend. If this product is going to become more popular it's automatically going much lower, maybe more like 5%, but given its voluntary nature and Strategy's opportunism, even if it isn't received well they could also do something like reduce to 3% or 0% and buy back shares to keep the $100 peg instead of depending on the dividend.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61924471
>>61924170
it's a scam and ponzi scheme don't buy it (I just bought more in my Roth today)

61924481
>>61924465
Is a turbo ponzi.

61926067
>>61924170
Saylor is going to have 1m+ BTC by the time all the banks start begging customers to custody with them.

Saylor is going to be the richest guy in the world.
He's never selling, only buying, forever

61926172
or i could just buy btc and btci

61926212
Do you hear that? That's the sound of future wholecoiners being vaporized

61926292
>>61924371
unironically LUNA 2.0 but worse

61926299
>>61924465
>buyback the shares
if you're suggesting they sell btc to do this you know this is how a deathspiral happens

61926327
>>61924170
good thread, what's the options play here

61926359
>>61924170
The big takeaway should be about how long banks have been fucking people with low interest rates on deposits

61926596
>>61926359
Anybody with two brain cells can finda a HYSA or buy a bond ETF

61928120
>>61924170
Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) keeps the STRC (Stretch) Variable Rate Series A Perpetual Preferred Stock distribution around 10% (ranging between 9% and 11.5% as of early 2026) primarily by utilizing a variable, monthly adjusted dividend rate designed to keep the security trading close to its $100 par value.
The company employs several active management mechanisms to maintain this stability and yield:
Monthly Dividend Adjustment: The Board of Directors adjusts the dividend rate monthly based on the five-day volume-weighted average price (VWAP) of the stock. If the stock price dips below $100 (e.g., during high volatility in Bitcoin), the company increases the dividend to make it more attractive to buyers and pull the price back to par.
At-the-Market (ATM) Offerings: Strategy uses an ATM program to issue new STRC shares, generally targeting a narrow band of $99–$101. This allows them to manage liquidity and support the price, with a $4.2 billion ATM program in place as of late 2025.
Bitcoin-Backed Overcollateralization: Each share of STRC is backed by Bitcoin at a ratio of roughly 5-to-1 (roughly $5 of BTC for every $1 of annual dividend). This immense collateral provides a cushion that reassures investors of the safety of the dividend, allowing for a lower required yield to maintain investor confidence.
Redemption/Call Rights: Strategy has the option to redeem (call) STRC shares at a set price (e.g., $101 per share plus accrued dividends) if the price rises significantly above par, preventing it from trading too high, or as part of active portfolio management.
Cash Buffer: To ensure consistent, high-yield payouts without needing to sell their Bitcoin treasury during down markets, Strategy has established a cash buffer, such as the $1.44 billion reserve announced in late 2025, specifically to fund preferred dividends

61928155
This shit has never worked, ever. It will crash and burn.

61928309
>>61926299
You might be a nice guy, but you're precisely the typical lazy ass commentator I was referring to.
In addition to
>selling bitcoin
they
>already have a cash reserve covering 2y worth of obligations
>can top it up by diluting common stock
>can top it up by selling more STR products
>can top it up by putting out more convertible notes, etc
So if you had ever looked in what they do you'd they can stomach another 1-2 years of bear market without doing anything at all, and then they have 3 fallbacks to generate more cash, and finally they can also sell bitcoin, which due to the cylical nature of this asset may not be a terrible idea either. Sometimes you sell, sometimes you buy, MSTR tries never to sell, but they are extremely opportunistic and can sell at an opportune time if is necessary to do this.

61928376
>>61928309
The fear around them selling as something catastrophic is exaggerated. If you boast of of being over collateralized, selling the collateral has to be an option on the table and as you pointed out they have several options before they'd have to sell to cover dividends.

61928415
>>61928376
Yes, and even if they do sell, they can do a single transaction to cover a year or so of obligations.
This might move the market -2% or so in the process, then -9% when they announce what they did and the inevitable FUD around MSTR selling takes hold, but the market will then have another year or so to calm down and forget about it until the next sale becomes necessary.
The MtGox and Germany government BTC dumps didn't make a sustained dent either, and those were higher amounts and weeks of FUD.

61928472
>>61928309
>already have a cash reserve covering 2y worth of obligations
thats not enough to buy $8bil strc back
>can top it up by diluting common stock
scenario strc unpegged i dont forsee mstr being above NAV
>can top it up by selling more STR products
obviously wouldnt work if strc is spiralling

im not saying its gonna happen soon im just saying a strc deathspiral can happen, his only realistic option to save it is selling btc but we both know thats gigabearish too

61928656
>>61928472
You're already backpedaling to saying it "can" happen.
You didn't address the idea of using a cash reserve that can be built up in good times. It took them a few weeks of mainly diluting the common stock (without much of a price impact) to build their current 2b+ reserve.
Even if they were to top it up using bitcoin sales they could likely also keep the duration of selling and price impact minimal.
>buy $8bil strc back
It's below $4b now. If they go higher, they collect fresh funds in the process, and the product becomes more established and the dividend probably goes down.
OP complained that they raised it to 11.5%, but the stock was already near $100, so they probably just raised it opportunistically to collect more money this week because they want to use the current low prices to buy more. They could likely also have reduced it to 9 or 10% without impacting the price much in the other direction.
In any case, any payments are voluntary and can be suspended/postponed or stopped entirely.
Yes, doing so would hurt the product, but the doomsday nonsense of an imagined necessity to panic-sell into a death spiral simply fails to acknowledge all options.
You can tell Strategy spends all day every day designing a bitcoin treasury and bear commenters don't.

The one thing that's true is that bitcoin halfway needs continue succeeding as a viable asset.
Whether that is in typical 4y cycle or supercycle style or something smoother.
If you think it can go to 0, every treasury company fails, but assuming that bitcoin is going to do at least halfway OK is very premise of buying any treasuries at all.

61928745
>>61924170
no thanks

61929542
>>61928656
bitcoin doesnt need to go anywhere near 0 for mstr to be fucked, we could crab mostly between $50k and $80k for the next 4 years and thats not even bearish

61930030
>>61926212
It's unfathomable for low-IQ no-coiners.
The STRC bitcoin printer is hoovering up 100's of bitcoin per hour of trading in a bear market. With the MSTR ATM going brrrrr alongside, Strategy will have over a million btc before the end of this year, possibly by Q3.
Feeling comfy rn in my ponzi onesie.

61930347
>>61929542
You just made that up like everyone who comments on MSTR because you're lazy. Obviously they'll be fine crabbing 4 years in that range.
The Germany + MtGox dump of 2024 showed that those entities were easily able to dump 110k coins on the market, much of it with ridiculous unplanned market selling, without making a dent for more than a month.
Strategy can likely easily pull 5-10b from bitcoin sales plus shares dilution in a few weeks without causing a lasting crash and it will be enough for many more years in addition to the 2 they alread have.

61930648
>>61926596
hSYA don’t even beat real inflation you goyboy

61930696
>>61930347
> easily able to dump 110k coins on the market without making a dent
and pray tell me, how much did saylor buy in 2024 -now you see the problem?

61930820
>>61930696
Just stop making up random nonsense.
But the fact that nearly 100% of anti-MSTR posts are nonsense like yours makes me quite bullish.
On the way up we'll be able to tell you opposite nonsense and you have no way of validating it and have to accept it. MSTR to acquire Nvidia. It's true because we say so you have to buy the top.

61930877
>>61924170
Yes.
Me.
This is literally free money as long as you sell before Bitcoin goes up by a lot again.
Saylor can see this going as long as he wants. Keeping the price around 95-100
9-11% return on capital
In order for it to cause you trouble, Bitcoin price would have to fall deeper than anyone would expect, which it won't because of that.

61930905
You know how Anons used to go stable coins? This is better. Much better.

61930943
>>61930877
>>61930905
There's something for everyone here, especially its function as tradfi gateway is underappreciated.
When MSTR was still into selling corporate bonds, they got lots of large banks and insurances to buy them. Because the bond market is huge, and bond asset managers found it interesting to buy a bond that moves like bitcoin.
STRC can pull a lot of funds from tradfi money markets as well. Steady, tax-free returns every month, growing history of confirmed ~$100 peg.
Then there's the stock of course.
And many cycle believers who still move a few billions might sit out the bear market in STRC instead of USDT to earn yield.

61930987
>>61930943
> who still move a few billions might sit it out the bear market in STRC instead of USDT
They do. And not only the big ones.

item.title: Does IQ make you more successful financially? — 43 replies — 30.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Does IQ make you more successful financially?

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-03-03 18:02 UTC

OP

Does IQ make you more successful financially?

Early replies (first 3)

61927833
>>61927814
No, but emotional intelligence does.

61927872
>>61927833
>emphaty
retard detected

61927878
>>61927814
>>61927833
>>61927872


now post her without make up and filters and 0.1sec clips

i'll wait

fucking yellow fever fags

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928387
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emotional_intelligence

61928394
What if somebody wants to learn the latin language and shit. Maybe they font want to optimize the tiktok.

61928410
>>61927814
Yes, everything else is cope and lies of low IQ people who really want to be high IQ and tells that kind of lies to them selfs.

61928414
Asian pussy makes you successful. That's why WMAF is the future.

61928426
>>61927833
>Motivation
All is vanity as the preacher said. I can do things though but my heart will not be in any of them
>Social skills
Yes, i have them but i hate people and choose to avoid them like the plague
>Empathy
Real intelligence entails high levels of empathy. I get you're sad but i won't cry with you because your cat has died. I don't expect you to cry if my father dies. I won't. It's the circle of life, Simba.
>Self awareness
I can read a room perfectly enough to know when my antisemitic jokes haven't landed as i expected
>Self regulation
Buzzword i don't know what it means. I can regulate and adjust myself according to the situation.

61928448
>>61927814
I have ~130IQ and i'm a poorfag NEET college dropout at 24. I have very little self-control or motivation so it's hard to get and hold a job, and I am very socially awkward so it's hard to make connections (the only way to get any skilled job nowadays), so i ended up an outcast. Many people in that range seem to have similar issues.

However, if you have 110-120IQ you probably will be "normal" enough to not be a weird outcast and smart enough to get ahead of most normalfags, and if you're >145IQ you're a genius and will probably end up being world-class at something.

I'm not trying to sadpost or get sympathy, it's just what I've observed.

61928455
>>61928448
Yea being king of the midwits is the best, you just crash out if you have le intelligence and realize we live in hell

61928672
>>61927833
Every billionaire is a psycho that eats the babies he just raped and the goyim be like
>duuuude just be nice and money will come to you :)

61928885
>>61928448
I'm sorry man. Whenever I meet academic people they seem to be not that bright. Often they can only do that one thing they are specialized and that's it. They just had that motivation to study hard but they are not that smart. Still they make make good money. They just fit into the system and profit from it.

61928893
>>61927814
IQ is simply the ceiling for skill and knowledge. It alone doesn't guarantee anything.

61929003
>>61928885
That's because academic people *aren't* that bright. The median IQ of college professors is only 128, only slightly higher than medical doctors (124).
>>61928448
I have a 161 IQ and I'm also a college dropout.
>if you're >145IQ you're a genius and will probably end up being world-class at something
I wouldn't say I'm world-class at anything except shitposting on 4chan, but I think the extra IQ means it's easy to do well without really trying. The place I was working had spent years and millions of dollars trying to solve some knotty problems and had made no progress despite having a PhD and several mensa members on the team. I happened to overhear some discussion about it in the lunchroom and before I finished my ham sandwich I had figured out the solution, which led to me getting promoted to the technical lead on that team and making pretty good money, a decade later I'm in the top 1% in net worth in the US, all for a whopping 5 minutes of actual thinking.

61929120
IQ doesn't exist.

The only thing it tests, is if you are cognitively retarded or not. But then again, basically any type of test can determine that.

The only reason people still talk about IQ is because people's fragile ego's and political dogmas hinge upon it. Not because it has any scientific merit.

61929203
>>61929120
>reeee this thing I don't have doesn't ackshually exist
It's funny because you think you're being clever about it, but it's all just so transparent and sad.

61929223
>>61929203
It's funny how you confirmed exactly what he said with your little emotional outburst.
I have no idea what my IQ is myself, never tested it. All the smart guys I know never tested their IQ either, if you care about it, that's already a sign you're an insecure midwit

61929256
>>61927814
how the fuck would i know

61929257
>>61929120
t. 94 IQ faggot who thought he was the next Einstein before taking the test

61929269
>>61929223
Actual smart guys were taken by their parents to a psychologist for IQ testing in grade school though, it's a necessary step for skipping grades and early graduation/college.

61929405
>>61929257
>>61929203
This is always the boring predictable response from losers and failures with no achievements in life, and no real talents. Just to bait me into revealing that I happened to score very highly on the IQ test.

Lack of introspection is a sign of low intelligence though. So naturally when you got *your* bullshit score, you didn't question it for a second, and proudly displayed it on your reddit profiles.

61929628
>>61929405
>So naturally when you got *your* bullshit score, you didn't question it for a second
this is evidence you never did an IQ test in your life

61929644
>>61929269
Why are you trying to proof he had no high IQ? You are proofing him again by doing it. "people's fragile ego's and political dogmas hinge upon it." :)

61929692
>>61927814
does pooping make women smell better?

61929814
Get a load of this young lady.
(temperance)

61930178
>>61927872
Its a jeet.
>hello saars pleas have the emphaty

61930196
>>61927878
she is not fat.
i simple cannot get hard with fat women.
even an average asian woman is 10x hotter than your average burger woman by virtue of not being disgustingly obese.

61930320
>>61928672
I'm opportunist and being nice pays off most of the time. They are ways to make money that require being asshole but being an asshole alone doesn't make any money. Some people always want to play against the rules but that's sailing against the wind and makes things unnecessary difficult. Most men are not willing to please a man to get rich. It doesn't need to be homoerotic. Just understanding what HE wants and giving it to him in exchange to the thing you want from him. It can be anything. Something simple as being nice or doing him a favor. Since I have a boyfriend I have learned a lot about men. They are just as emotional as women but try to hide it all the time. If you can read them anyway you know weather they like or dislike something you do. You can read if they are emotionally attached to you, to know if you can ask them for a favor.

Bullying or trolling people on 4chan doesn't make any money but people do it anyway because they want to feel good. They don't think about weather each of there actions in life will cost or gain them money long term. There only measurement is weather it feels good in the moment.

61930333
>>61927833
sounds gay

61930366
>>61927881
There is a difference between empathy and compassion. If you don't have empathy you are kind of retarded.

61930381
>>61930366
^^^

61930579
>>61927833
All five of these are so critical that lacking one can ruin your entire life. It's the term "emotional intelligence" that's really gay and ruins the whole thing. Swapping the word emotional for something like behavioral or social would be more well received.

61930771
>>61930579
That's a male problem and not the words fault. It's job is to be true, not to be seen as straight and be popular among men.

Open thread • Replies: 84 • Updated: 2026-03-03 15:59 UTC

61900872
>>61899833
I'll just keep buying small amounts of it for the rest of my life, and see what happens.

61900948
>>61899833
i believe in shib

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61919529
Shib is fucking dead

61919535
>>61919529
yeah, kinda is
Thankfully i still got in early enough that there was never any chance of not making a profit despite missing out on the big gains through greed but there's nothing but a slow bleed right now and i've seen nothing that means it might pump again

61921116
Bro imagine buying that

61921137
Not today

61921143
No such thing as a free lunch

61921144
>>61899833
none of these shitcoins are gonna do big movements again retard

61921169
>>61921144
No such thing as a free lunch

61921558
Just bought some more boys, let's go

61921644
>>61901257

61921961
>>61921558

61921988
>>61921644

61922004
kek turd killer still being shilled here in 2026
lmfao nice AI pics btw

61922014
>>61922004

61922070
>>61922004
You have 2 more weeks wagie

61922078
